sentence_rhythm: Mixed rhythm with declarative statements punctuating longer explanatory passages; tends toward direct, purposeful sentences that build momentum during crisis moments; uses fragments for emphasis
dialogue_style: Speeches carry weight and formality befitting Starfleet tradition; subtext emerges through what characters choose to acknowledge versus avoid; rallying cries and collective affirmations punctuate key moments
scene_openings: Often begin with situational framing—establishing the tactical or emotional stakes before introducing character action; context-setting through acknowledgment of dire circumstances
pacing: Tension built through accumulation of problems and resource scarcity; release comes through moments of collective resolve or decisive leadership choices; crisis drives forward momentum
vocabulary_register: Accessible military-institutional language; Starfleet terminology used naturally; occasional literary or philosophical references woven in; formal during speeches, pragmatic during tactical discussions
description_density: Sparse on physical description, focused on functional details—sensor status, defense capabilities, crew state; emotional atmosphere conveyed through collective mood rather than elaborate scene-painting
character_interiority: Moderate; leaders shown weighing options and recalling relevant knowledge; internal reflection tends toward practical problem-solving rather than extended emotional exploration
conflict_style: External threats force internal choices; conflict resolved through leadership decisions, negotiation consideration, and collective commitment; surrender explicitly rejected in favor of agency
recurring_motifs: the old giving way to necessity
what_to_avoid: excessive technical jargon without emotional grounding
dismissing the weight of institutional symbols
